notes: The central relationship of the entire canon. Holmes and Watson meet in early 1881 and share lodgings at 221B Baker Street. Watson serves as Holmes's companion, assistant, and chronicler — his admiring but honest narration is the frame through which the world knows Holmes. The relationship is asymmetrical: Holmes is the genius, Watson the capable everyman. But Watson's warmth, loyalty, and willingness to follow Holmes into danger make him irreplaceable. Holmes, characteristically, expresses his regard obliquely — through trust, through inclusion, through the fact that Watson is the only person Holmes consistently chooses to have near him. Watson's marriages interrupt but never permanently sever the partnership.
